# Analysis summary

**Live demo**: `https://justwatch.australscience.com`  
**Data note**: demo uses **fake / synthetic data generated by AI** (portfolio-safe).

## The problem

A UI update was expected to increase engagement, but engagement **declined**. Internal analytics showed **where** users dropped off, but not **why**.

## What I did (Data Analyst)

- Integrated **internal engagement** signals with **external social feedback** to connect behavior with perception.
- Built a thread-level feedback dataset (post + comments) and applied cleaning/validation.
- Ran sentiment + topic classification and designed business-aligned metrics beyond “basic sentiment”.

## Key insight

- **Negative sentiment increased within 24–48 hours post-release**.
- Main drivers: **navigation**, **content discoverability**, and UX friction.
- Perception trends aligned with engagement drop, explaining the **root cause** behind user behavior.

## Impact

- Product team prioritized specific UX fixes faster.
- Growth team gained an early warning signal to monitor release risk.
- Created a reusable post-release monitoring framework (metrics + dashboard + documentation).
